chunks.text: The College of Engineering held a groundbreaking ceremony for its new Engineering Center on April 21. The new building, which is scheduled for completion in 2025, will be a hub for student collaboration, innovation, and entrepreneurship. The building will serve as a space for students across different disciplines and perspectives.
chunks.question: What is the purpose of the new Engineering Center at Berkeley?
chunks.answer: A hub for student collaboration and entrepreneurship.

chunks.text: EE Prof. Alberto Sangiovanni-Vincentelli has won the 15th BBVA Foundation Frontiers of Knowledge Award in Information and Communication Technologies. He was cited 'for radically transforming the design of the chips that power today's electronic devices, giving rise to the modern semiconductor industry.' Prof. Sangiovanni-Vincentelli transformed chip design in three fundamental ways.
chunks.question: What award did Alberto Sangiovanni-Vincentelli win?

chunks.text: EECS alumna Putri Karunia (B.S. '19) co-founded Typedream, a 2022 Forbes 30-Under-30 Enterprise Tech company. She is profiled in an article highlighting that women in tech startups increase success and profitability. Karunia, raised in Indonesia, graduated cum laude from Berkeley.
chunks.question: What company did Putri Karunia co-found?

chunks.text: CS alumna Medha Kothari (B.A. '20) is featured in a podcast episode titled 'Blockchain for the People.' As a student, she founded she256, a non-profit aiming to increase diversity in blockchain. Kothari is currently a Research Partner at Variant.
chunks.question: What organization did Medha Kothari found?

chunks.text: CS Prof. Ali Ghodsi is the inaugural speaker for 'The Tale of a Success' entrepreneurship series, hosted by Iranian student associations. Ghodsi is the co-founder and CEO of Databricks, an enterprise software startup valued at over $28 billion.
chunks.question: Who is the CEO of Databricks?
chunks.answer: Ali Ghodsi, a CS professor at Berkeley.

chunks.text: CS Prof. Joseph Hellerstein was named a Datanami 2021 Person to Watch. He is the chief strategy officer and co-founder of Trifacta, which markets data preparation technology based on Data Wrangler, a tool he developed in the RISELab.
chunks.question: What company did Joseph Hellerstein co-found?
chunks.answer: Trifacta, a data preparation technology company.

chunks.text: Databricks, a cloud startup founded by Berkeley faculty and alumni including Ali Ghodsi, Scott Shenker, and Ion Stoica, raised $1 billion in Series G funding. The company has a valuation of over $28 billion and is based in San Francisco.
chunks.question: How much funding did Databricks raise in Series G?

chunks.text: Ambidextrous, co-founded in 2018 by CS Prof. Ken Goldberg and his team, won the inaugural Silicon Valley Robotics 'Good Robot' Excellence Award. The company focuses on robotic automation and innovation in industrial applications.
chunks.question: What award did Ambidextrous win?

chunks.text: EECS Prof. Emeritus Michael Stonebraker won the 2020 NEC C&C Prize for pioneering contributions to relational database systems. The prize recognizes outstanding work in semiconductors, computers, and telecommunications.
chunks.question: Why did Michael Stonebraker win the C&C Prize?

chunks.text: CS alumna Cecilia Aragon (Ph.D. '04) wrote a memoir titled 'Flying Free,' describing how she overcame discrimination and fear of heights to become the first Latina pilot on the U.S. Unlimited Aerobatic Team. Her advisors were Shankar Sastry and Marti Hearst.
chunks.question: What is Cecilia Aragon known for?
chunks.answer: First Latina pilot on U.S. aerobatic team.
